message:
chore(beads): close br-r37-c1-3lqi6 (DiGraph.reverse iteration order)

Fixed in commit 37679bb.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -9,7 +9,7 @@
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-2hrfs","title":"find_cycle (undirected) edge-tuple direction drifts from nx","description":"fnx.find_cycle on an undirected graph yields edges in algorithm-canonical orientation, but nx yields edges in DFS-traversal direction (which depends on adj-iteration order).

Repro:
  edges = [('c','d'), ('a','b'), ('b','c'), ('d','e'), ('a','c')]
  fnx.find_cycle(g) -> [('c','a'), ('a','b'), ('b','c')]
  nx.find_cycle(gx) -> [('c','b'), ('b','a'), ('a','c')]

Edge tuples are reversed and the cycle is traversed in the opposite DFS direction. Fix: delegate find_cycle to nx for undirected graphs (the directed case already matches).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T12:59:04.011873621Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T13:08:43.076611064Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T13:08:43.074643528Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-2vdtt","title":"strongly_connected_components order disagrees with nx on multi-component DiGraphs","description":"fnx.strongly_connected_components currently materialises Rust's SCC output and applies reversed() to match nx. The reversed() flip happens to match small-symmetric cases but is wrong on inputs where Rust and nx disagree on component-discovery start node.

Repro:
  edges = [('c','d'),('a','b'),('b','c'),('d','a'),('c','a'),('e','f'),('f','e')]
  fnx -> [{'f','e'}, {'a','b','c','d'}]
  nx  -> [{'a','b','c','d'}, {'f','e'}]
  edges = [('a','b'),('b','a'),('c','d'),('d','c')]
  fnx -> [{'c','d'}, {'b','a'}]
  nx  -> [{'b','a'}, {'c','d'}]

Fix: delegate strongly_connected_components to nx for parity (Rust + reversed() heuristic is unsound).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T12:59:39.307393335Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T13:02:29.392395805Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T13:02:29.390417839Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-3dxfn","title":"parity: all_pairs_dijkstra_path / all_pairs_dijkstra_path_length outer-key iteration order doesn't match nx","description":"fnx.all_pairs_dijkstra_path and fnx.all_pairs_dijkstra_path_length yield outer-key (source) iteration in arbitrary Rust internal order (e.g. ['a','e','c','d','b']) instead of nx's node-insertion order (['c','d','a','b','e']). Drop-in code that iterates 'for source, dists in result.items(): ...' breaks. Same iteration-order family fixed for triangles/core_number/etc. Note: outer keys for all_pairs_shortest_path[_length] already match nx; only dijkstra variants need the fix.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T10:35:36.535013167Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T10:38:03.955576532Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T10:38:03.953597595Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 40d5417. all_pairs_dijkstra_path / _length outer keys now iterate in node-insertion order matching nx. 6 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"br-r37-c1-3lqi6","title":"DiGraph.reverse() / fnx.reverse() edge + adj iteration order drifts from nx","description":"fnx.reverse() (and DiGraph.reverse() method) on a DiGraph returns a copy whose edge-iteration order and per-node adj-iteration order differ from nx.

Repro:
  edges = [('a','b'),('b','c'),('c','d')]  # path
  fnx.reverse(dg).edges() -> [('c','b'),('d','c'),('b','a')]
  nx.reverse(dgx).edges() -> [('b','a'),('c','b'),('d','c')]

  edges = [(0,1),(1,2),(2,3),(3,0)]  # cycle
  fnx -> [(1,0),(3,2),(0,3),(2,1)]
  nx  -> [(0,3),(1,0),(2,1),(3,2)]

  edges = [('c','d'),('a','b'),('b','c'),('d','a'),('c','a')]
  fnx adj['a'] = ['d', 'c']
  nx adj['a']  = ['c', 'd']

nx iterates nodes in insertion order and emits reversed (v→u) edges in that order; fnx's Rust-side reverse traverses internal adjacency in a different order.

The copy=False path matches. The copy=True path (default for both function and method) drifts.

Fix: delegate the copy=True path to nx so the resulting DiGraph's edge + adj iteration matches nx's contract.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T13:56:39.907591704Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T13:57:26.964591470Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"br-r37-c1-3lqi6","title":"DiGraph.reverse() / fnx.reverse() edge + adj iteration order drifts from nx","description":"fnx.reverse() (and DiGraph.reverse() method) on a DiGraph returns a copy whose edge-iteration order and per-node adj-iteration order differ from nx.

Repro:
  edges = [('a','b'),('b','c'),('c','d')]  # path
  fnx.reverse(dg).edges() -> [('c','b'),('d','c'),('b','a')]
  nx.reverse(dgx).edges() -> [('b','a'),('c','b'),('d','c')]

  edges = [(0,1),(1,2),(2,3),(3,0)]  # cycle
  fnx -> [(1,0),(3,2),(0,3),(2,1)]
  nx  -> [(0,3),(1,0),(2,1),(3,2)]

  edges = [('c','d'),('a','b'),('b','c'),('d','a'),('c','a')]
  fnx adj['a'] = ['d', 'c']
  nx adj['a']  = ['c', 'd']

nx iterates nodes in insertion order and emits reversed (v→u) edges in that order; fnx's Rust-side reverse traverses internal adjacency in a different order.

The copy=False path matches. The copy=True path (default for both function and method) drifts.

Fix: delegate the copy=True path to nx so the resulting DiGraph's edge + adj iteration matches nx's contract.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T13:56:39.907591704Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T13:59:41.487970050Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T13:59:41.485884732Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-4elbw","title":"parity: 4 more functions raise NotImplemented/NetworkXError with custom messages instead of nx's standard wording","description":"Continuation of br-r37-c1-yf4tf — 4 more functions diverge from nx's exact error messages: (1) condensation on undirected raises 'strongly_connected_components is not defined for undirected graphs. Use connected_components instead.'; nx raises 'not implemented for undirected type'. (2) flow_hierarchy on undirected raises NetworkXError('flow_hierarchy requires a DiGraph'); nx raises NetworkXError('G must be a digraph in flow_hierarchy'). (3) in_degree_centrality and (4) out_degree_centrality on undirected raise custom 'is not defined for undirected graphs.' messages; nx raises 'not implemented for undirected type'. Drop-in code matching the exact text breaks. Fix: align all four to nx's exact wording.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T09:28:50.121233008Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T09:32:37.902270771Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T09:32:37.900331589Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 35bb5d3. strongly_connected_components/number_strongly/kosaraju/condensation/in_degree_centrality/out_degree_centrality/flow_hierarchy now raise with nx's exact error messages. 17 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-5nop9","title":"parity: bfs_labeled_edges public signature should be (G, sources) matching networkx","description":"fnx.bfs_labeled_edges has signature (G, sources=None, sort_neighbors=None, *, source=None) — three problems vs nx (G, sources): sources is required in nx but optional here, sort_neighbors is not part of upstream signature, source is a keyword-only legacy alias. Even though both shapes appear to function, the public signature divergence is observable via inspect.signature, breaks parameter introspection used by typed wrappers, and means typer-style strict argument routing breaks. Tighten the wrapper to (G, sources) matching nx; if downstream callers pass sort_neighbors/source those are unsupported by nx and should fail loudly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T01:09:39.386409859Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T03:16:47.912406594Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T03:16:47.910554548Z","close_reason":"Shipped in bf5195e: bfs_labeled_edges sig tightened to (G, sources). Removed sort_neighbors and source= legacy params. 3 existing tests still pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-5npb6","title":"parity: Graph class method signatures hidden by @wraps decorator on top of Rust-bound methods","description":"Many Graph/DiGraph/MultiGraph/MultiDiGraph methods are wrapped via @wraps(rust_impl) — the Rust impl has signature () so functools.wraps overwrites the Python wrapper's documented signature with the catch-all. inspect.signature(g.to_directed) returns () instead of (self, as_view=False). 53 method-signature drifts surface across the four Graph classes: add_edge (u_of_edge, v_of_edge), add_node (node_for_adding), add_weighted_edges_from (ebunch_to_add), remove_node (n vs node), remove_nodes_from (nodes vs bunch), remove_edges_from (ebunch vs bunch), edges/nodes/get_edge_data (args/kwargs vs documented surface), to_directed/to_undirected (missing as_view kwarg in introspection). Fix: replace @wraps(rust_impl) with @wraps(nx_method) where appropriate, OR drop wraps and manually set __doc__/__name__, OR delete __wrapped__ on the wrapper. Add parity tests asserting inspect.signature(method) matches nx for all four classes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T05:14:17.351791844Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T05:17:19.848195304Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T05:17:19.846339454Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 2026bc3. Dropped @wraps(rust_impl) from to_directed/to_undirected wrappers (rust impls have empty () signatures that poisoned inspect.signature via __wrapped__). Now inspect.signature(g.to_directed) returns nx's (as_view=False) and DiGraph.to_undirected returns (reciprocal=False, as_view=False). 11 parity tests freeze the contract. Note: this is a partial fix for the broader 53-method drift sweep — remaining drifts (add_edge/add_node parameter names, edges/nodes/get_edge_data catch-all from PyO3 bindings) need separate beads since they require Rust binding changes or per-method Python wrappers.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
